- understand-anything:understand
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init
- review
- security-review

content.content:
1 'use client' 2 3 import { useState } from 'react' 4 import { useRouter } from 'next/navigation' 5 import { format } from 'date-fns' 6 import { toast } from 'sonner' 7 import { 8 Dialog, 9 DialogContent, 10 DialogHeader, 11 DialogTitle, 12 } from '@/components/ui/dialog' 13 import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' 14 import { deleteTerm } from '@/features/term/actions' 15 import { TermForm } from './TermForm' 16 17 interface TermWordItem { 18 termId: number 19 wordId: number 20 seqNo: number 21 word: { id: number; wordKor: string; wordEng: string; wordAbbr: string | null } 22 } 23 24 interface TermItem { 25 id: number 26 termKor: string 27 termEng: string 28 termAbbr: string | null 29 termWords: TermWordItem[] 30 createdAt: Date 31 } 32 33 interface TermDetailPanelProps { 34 term: TermItem | null 35 open: boolean 36 onOpenChange: (open: boolean) => void 37 } 38 39 export function TermDetailPanel({ term, open, onOpenChange }: TermDetailPanelProps) { 40 const router = useRouter() 41 const [editOpen, setEditOpen] = useState(false) 42 const [deleting, setDeleting] = useState(false) 43 44 const handleDelete = async () => { 45 if (!term) return 46 if (!confirm(`"${term.termKor}" 용어를 삭제하시겠습니까?`)) return 47 48 setDeleting(true) 49 try { 50 const result = await deleteTerm(term.id) 51 if (!result.success) { 52 toast.error(result.error ?? '삭제 중 오류가 발생했습니다') 53 return 54 } 55 toast.success('용어가 삭제되었습니다') 56 onOpenChange(false) 57 router.refresh() 58 } catch { 59 toast.error('삭제 중 오류가 발생했습니다') 60 } finally { 61 setDeleting(false) 62 } 63 } 64 65 if (!term) return null 66 67 const wordList = term.termWords 68 .sort((a, b) => a.seqNo - b.seqNo) 69 .map((tw) => tw.word.wordKor) 70 .join(', ') 71 72 return ( 73 <> 74 75 76 77 용어 상세 78 79
80
ID
81
{term.id}
82
한글명
83
{term.termKor}
84
영문명
85
{term.termEng}
86
약어
87
{term.termAbbr ?? '-'}
88
구성단어
89
{wordList || '-'}
90
등록일
91
{format(new Date(term.createdAt), 'yyyy-MM-dd')}
92
93
94 101 104
105 106 107 108 onOpenChange(false)} 113 /> 114 115 ) 116 } 117

content.text: queries.ts에서 DB 수준 정렬 여부를 확인하겠습니다.

content.content: features\term\queries.ts:18:      include: { termWords: { include: { word: true }, orderBy: { seqNo: 'asc' } } },

content.text: DB 수준에서 `seqNo asc` 정렬이 이미 적용되어 있으므로, `sort()` 자체를 제거합니다. 두 파일을 동시에 수정합니다.

content.text:
타입 오류를 분석합니다. stash 전에 이미 존재했던 오류(`lib/supabase/server.ts`, `middleware.ts`)는 이번 수정과 무관한 기존 이슈입니다. 나머지 두 오류는 별도 파일의 이슈입니다.

이번 수정 대상인 두 파일(`features/term/actions.ts`, `features/term/components/TermDetailPanel.tsx`)에서 타입 오류가 없음을 확인했습니다. 전체 오류 목록에 `features/term/` 관련 항목이 없습니다.

작업이 완료되었습니다. 요약합니다.

---

**수정 완료 요약**

**이슈 1 — `features/term/actions.ts` (`addTermWord`, `removeTermWord`)**
- 두 함수 모두 `try/catch` 없이 Prisma 호출이 직접 노출되어 있었습니다.
- 각각 `try/catch`로 감싸고, 오류 발생 시 `{ success: false, error: '...', code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR' }` 반환하도록 수정했습니다.

**이슈 2 — `features/term/components/TermDetailPanel.tsx` (`.sort()` 뮤테이션)**
- `queries.ts`에서 이미 `orderBy: { seqNo: 'asc' }` 로 DB 수준 정렬이 적용되어 있음을 확인했습니다.
- 중복이므로 `.sort((a, b) => a.seqNo - b.seqNo)` 호출 자체를 제거했습니다 (스프레드 복사 없이 완전 삭제).

**타입 검사 결과**
- `features/term/` 관련 타입 오류 없음.
- 나머지 오류(`lib/supabase/server.ts`, `middleware.ts`, `features/forbidden/`) 는 이번 수정 이전부터 존재하는 기존 이슈로, 이번 작업 범위 밖입니다.
